{"step_by_step_thinking": "The question asks about the most commonly used tooth numbering system in dental books. Among the options, the Universal Numbering System is widely recognized and used in dental textbooks, clinical practice, and educational materials. It provides a simple, consistent, and easy-to-apply method for identifying teeth, especially in the context of treatment planning and documentation. The FDI system (International System) is used in international settings and for dental records, but it is not the most commonly used in general dental books. The Zigmondy and Palmar system is a less common, older system, primarily used in some specific dental literature. The Grid System is not a standard tooth numbering system; it is more related to visual aids or diagnostic tools (e.g., Amsler grid). Therefore, based on standard dental education and literature, the Universal Numbering System is the most commonly used in books.", "answer_choice": "A"}
